title: Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion
author: Robert Cialdini
content: “A well-known principle of human behavior says that when we ask someone to do us a favor we will be more successful if we will provide a reason.”
action: Record: Rationalization is a tool; providing a logical wrapper for a request significantly increases the probability of compliance.

title: The Art of Seduction
author: Robert Greene
content: “The first step to seduction is to learn to cast a spell... by focusing on the target’s needs and desires.”
action: Record: Influence is not about your output; it is about calibrating your signal to the internal psychological requirements of the target.

title: Propaganda
author: Edward Bernays
content: “The conscious and intelligent manipulation of the organized habits and opinions of the masses is an important element in democratic society.”
action: Record: Public opinion is a manufactured product; understand the delivery mechanisms of information to shape reality before the target does.

title: Games People Play
author: Eric Berne
content: “Every game is basically dishonest, and the pay-off must be dramatic.”
action: Record: Identify the 'script' or 'game' an individual is playing in a social interaction to bypass their defensive posturing.

title: Social Engineering: The Art of Human Hacking
author: Christopher Hadnagy
content: “If you can make a person believe that you are who you say you are, then you can make them do almost anything.”
action: Record: Identity and context are the primary exploits; control the context, and you control the authorization level of the interaction.
